Makefile for building and managing the JerryScript target on Mbed OS 5 with configurable options.

# Purpose
This Makefile is used to automate the build process for a project that targets mbed boards. It allows users to specify the target board via the command line using the `make BOARD=[mbed board name]` command. The file defines several variables and flags, such as `HEAPSIZE`, `DEBUG`, `NO_JS`, and `MBED_VERBOSE`, which control the build configuration and verbosity. The `MBED_CLI_FLAGS` variable aggregates command-line options for the mbed CLI, including source directories, build directories, and compiler options. The Makefile includes targets for building the entire project (`all`), creating a library (`library`), cleaning build artifacts (`clean`), converting JavaScript files to C headers (`js2c`), and generating source files (`source/pins.cpp`). It also manages dependencies and configurations for the mbed environment, such as setting the mbed root and toolchain, and deploying necessary libraries.
